item.title: McGregor injures leg in UFC return, referee stops fight
Lack of focus from the start cost us the game - Seruvakula
Argentina beat Switzerland 3-1 after extra time to set up World Cup semi-final against England
item.description: Defending champions, Argentina booked their place in the FIFA World Cup semi finals after defeating Switzerland 3-1 following extra time in their quarter-final clash. The two teams were locked at 1-all at the end of regulation time before Argentina scored twice in extra time to secure the victory.
item.content:encoded:
Defending champions, Argentina booked their place in the FIFA World Cup semi finals after defeating Switzerland 3-1 following extra time in their quarter-final clash.

The two teams were locked at 1-all at the end of regulation time before Argentina scored twice in extra time to secure the victory.

Argentina had taken an early lead through Alexis Mac Allister before Switzerland fought back with an equaliser from Dan Ndoye in the second half, forcing the match into extra time.

Mac Allister, Julian Alvarez and Lautaro Martinez scored for Argentina while Dan Ndoye scored for Switzerland.

Switzerland also suffered when Breel Embolo was shown a red card in the secod half.

In the semi finals, France will play Spain at 7am on Wednesday, while Argentina will face England at 7am on Thursday.

item.title: Rewa, Labasa, Navua and Ba record wins in Extra Premier League matches
item.description: Rewa, Labasa, Navua and Ba recorded victories in today's Round 13 Extra Premier League matches, while the Babasiga Lions continue

item.title: Basic errors cost us the game against England: Ikanivere
item.description: FIJI Water Flying Fijians captain Tevita Ikanivere says basic errors and ill-discipline cost Fiji dearly in their heavy defeat to England. Speaking after the 73-8 loss, Ikanivere says the team is disappointed with the result, admitting they did not execute the basics well enough and gave England too much possession.
item.content:encoded:
FIJI Water Flying Fijians captain Tevita Ikanivere says basic errors and ill-discipline cost Fiji dearly in their heavy defeat to England.

Speaking after the 73-8 loss, Ikanivere says the team is disappointed with the result, admitting they did not execute the basics well enough and gave England too much possession.

Insert: Ikanivere on decisions – 12th July 26

Despite the result, Ikanivere believes Fiji's scrum was better than it was against Wales, but says the referee made the decisions as they saw them.

The Flying Fijians captain says the players now have to look at themselves, improve, and be ready for next week's match.

Ikanivere also thanked the fans for their support, saying it was Fiji's biggest crowd in the UK so far and that the team appreciated everyone who turned out in numbers.

The Flying Fijians will now turn their focus to their match against Scotland, kicking off at 1.10am next Sunday at Scottish Gas Murrayfield.

item.title: Labasa out to strengthen lead against Nadi as EPL title race heats up
item.description: Extra Premier League table leaders, Labasa will look to strengthen their position at the top of the standings when they take on Na

item.title: England edge Norway in extra time to reach FIFA World Cup semi finals
item.description: England have edged Norway 2-1 in extra time this morning in Miami to book their place in the FIFA World Cup semi finals. Jude Bellingham scored both goals for England.
item.content:encoded:
England have edged Norway 2-1 in extra time this morning in Miami to book their place in the FIFA World Cup semi finals.

Jude Bellingham scored both goals for England.

It was a heartbreaking moment for the Norway team and their fans.

The quarter-final encounter was closely contested, with one goal from each side ruled out for fouls during the match.

England will face the winner of the last quarter final between Argentina and Switzerland.

Argentina will take on Switzerland at 1pm.

item.title: Kadavu stuns Suva 17-7 in Skipper Cup warm-up
item.description: Kadavu claimed a 17-7 victory over Suva in a warm-up match as both teams continue preparations for the upcoming Skipper Cup competition. The Kadavu side has been in camp throughout the off-season, with players training both in Suva and at the team's base in Namalata, Kadavu, as they gear up for their historic debut in Fiji's premier provincial rugby competition.
item.content:encoded:
Kadavu claimed a 17-7 victory over Suva in a warm-up match as both teams continue preparations for the upcoming Skipper Cup competition.

The Kadavu side has been in camp throughout the off-season, with players training both in Suva and at the team's base in Namalata, Kadavu, as they gear up for their historic debut in Fiji's premier provincial rugby competition.

A major boost for Kadavu this season is the inclusion of former Fiji 7s and Super Rugby player John Stewart.

Stewart has been assisting the squad during their training sessions in Suva, bringing valuable experience and leadership to the team.

Having captained Suva on numerous occasions, Stewart has impressed with his composure, leadership, and maturity on the field.

Leading the side is captain Vaione Tegu, who played a key role in guiding Kadavu to the Vanua Trophy title last year.

Tegu has continued to work closely with the players as they prepare for the province's first-ever appearance in the Skipper Cup.

Current Fiji Airways Fiji 7s half back Terio Veilawa is also playing for his provincial side.

Kadavu will play Nadi in Kings Charles Park in Nadi in round one of the competition on the 1st of August.

item.title: Lomax reaches 50-Test milestone as All Blacks make 5 changes for Italy's match
item.description: Tyrel Lomax will earn his 50th Test cap as the All Blacks make five changes for tonight's Nations Championship clash against Italy
